parameters.name: mode
parameters.label: Mode
parameters.description: Controls whether the contents are taken from the Text parameter or from a Specification / .

parameters.name: text
parameters.label: Text
parameters.description: This parameter is the data source for the text that is displayed in the panel. The value may be a number, string, or a reference to an external data source using a python or bind expression. When the

parameters.name: specdat
parameters.label: Specification DAT
parameters.description: A path to a that is the source of the text data when in Specification mode. The table should have 3 columns labeled x, y and text in their first rows. Each row after that will display one line of text

parameters.name: specchop
parameters.label: Specification CHOP
parameters.description: A path to a that can provide numerical data when in Specification mode. Channels named x and y can be used to position the text data specified in the Specificaction . Each data row in the should have

parameters.name: type
parameters.label: Type
parameters.description: The Type parameter controls how the value is interpreted and as well as what formatting and editing options are available.

parameters.name: formatting
parameters.label: Formatting
parameters.description: This parameter controls how the source text is is formatted and displayed inside the panel. The options available will change depending on the selected mode. For example, in Float mode there are optio

parameters.name: customformatting
parameters.label: Custom Formatting
parameters.description: This parameter provides the formatting string for custom modes like Python F-String and C++ Fmt. In both cases, the source text is defined inside curly braces ( {} ) while text outside the braces is t

parameters.name: precision
parameters.label: Precision
parameters.description: Used in Float mode to set the number of decimal places.

parameters.name: thousandsseparator
parameters.label: Thousands Separator
parameters.description: Used in Float and Integer mode to add a separator between thousands to make it easier to read large numbers. For example 1,000,000

parameters.name: framerate
parameters.label: Framerate
parameters.description: Sets the number of frames per second when formatting integers into time codes.

parameters.name: formatcodes
parameters.label: Formatting Codes
parameters.description: Enables the use of formatting directives in the text. For example, the code {#color(255, 0, 0);} will turn all text afterwards on that line red. See for all available codes.

parameters.name: escapeseq
parameters.label: Parse Escape Sequences
parameters.description: When enabled, '\t' sequences will be converted to tabs and '\r' or '\n' to line breaks. This feature is only enabled when using the Specification . For Text mode, python expressions should be used whe

parameters.name: smartpunct
parameters.label: Smart Punctuation
parameters.description: Enables the use of smart punctuation. For example, pairs of quotes will be replaced with appropriate angled quotes, 3 periods will be replaced with an ellipses character and two hypens will become an

parameters.name: wordwrap
parameters.label: Word Wrap
parameters.description: Enables word wrap and can only be used when the 'Type' parameter above is set to 'Multi Line'. Word wrap will split single lines of text onto multiple lines when they extend past the edge of the panel

parameters.name: editmode
parameters.label: Edit Mode
parameters.description: Controls whether the text can be editing directly in the viewer.

parameters.name: shiftenter
parameters.label: Shift-Enter for New Line
parameters.description: In multiline mode, when this option is enabled, pressing Enter will complete editing and save the value and users must hold Shift and Enter if they wish to create a new line.

parameters.name: dragdropmode
parameters.label: Drag Drop Behaviour
parameters.description: This parameter controls what happens when a user drags something onto an active panel.

parameters.name: placeholdertext
parameters.label: Placeholder Text
parameters.description: This parameter is the data source for the text that is displayed in the panel when there is no text given. Similar to a hint text.

parameters.name: readingdirection
parameters.label: Reading Direction
parameters.description: Use this parameter to control whether the text is displayed from left-to-right (LTR) or from right-to-left (RTL). Bidirectional layout is also supported when LTR characters are detected when using RTL

parameters.name: fieldfocus
parameters.label: Focus Order
parameters.description: A path to a containing a list of component paths that is used to control which panel receives the focus when a user hits TAB or Shift+Tab during editing.

parameters.name: callbacks
parameters.label: Callbacks DAT
parameters.description: The path of a Text containing python callback functions that can be used to react to different events in the viewer.

parameters.name: legacyfontselection
parameters.label: Legacy Font Selection
parameters.description: Enable to select the font using the legacy system of bold and italic toggles. In the new system, bold and italic options can be chosen using the typeface parameter when available.

parameters.name: font
parameters.label: Font
parameters.description: Select the font to be used from the dropdown menu. Available fonts are those that have been registered with the operating system. There may be a delay when selecting fonts that have not been used befo

parameters.name: fontfile
parameters.label: Font File
parameters.description: Specify a font file to be used for rendering the text. This option can be used for fonts that are not registered with the operating system and do not appear in the drop down menu.

parameters.name: typeface
parameters.label: Typeface
parameters.description: Select a specific typeface for the current font e.g. Bold, Regular, Italic, etc. Not applicable in legacy font mode.

parameters.name: bold
parameters.label: Bold
parameters.description: Display the text in .

parameters.name: italic
parameters.label: Italic
parameters.description: Display the text in italics.

parameters.name: scaletofit
parameters.label: Scale Text to Fit
parameters.description: Automatically adjust the font size to fit inside the panel.

parameters.name: fontsize
parameters.label: Font Size
parameters.description: Set the size of the font. By default the font is defined in resolution-independent panel units that will be rendered to a variable number of pixels depending on DPI settings of the output window (e.g.

parameters.name: fontsizeunits
parameters.label: Font Size Units
parameters.description: Determines how the font size is interpretted. The default is resolution-independent panel units.

parameters.name: tracking
parameters.label: Tracking
parameters.description: Sets the horizontal spacing between characters, where 0 is default spacing, > 0 is increased spacing and < 0 is decreased spacing.

parameters.name: skew
parameters.label: Skew
parameters.description: Tilts the top of the characters forwards or backwards relative to the baseline.

parameters.name: horzstretch
parameters.label: Horz Stretch
parameters.description: Horizontally stretch the characters relative to their current alignment.

parameters.name: linespacing
parameters.label: Line Spacing
parameters.description: The spacing between lines when using multiline mode. The value is a multiplier of the default spacing which is defined as 1.2 * font size.

parameters.name: fontalpha
parameters.label: Font Alpha
parameters.description: The blending value used to display the text over the background. 1 is opaque while 0 is transparent.

parameters.name: customselectcolor
parameters.label: Custom Select Color
parameters.description: Enable this parameter to define a custom color used for the selection bar when editing the text. When disabled, the selection color will be automatically chosen to maximize contrast between the backgr

parameters.name: dropshadow
parameters.label: Drop Shadow
parameters.description: Draw a drop shadow under the text.

parameters.name: dropshadowalpha
parameters.label: Drop Shadow Alpha
parameters.description: The blending value used to display the shadow over the background. 1 is opaque while 0 is transparent. Premultiplied by Font Alpha.

parameters.name: dropshadowoffsetunits
parameters.label: Drop Shadow Offset Units
parameters.description: Determine the units used by the Drop Shadow Offset parameter.

parameters.name: textoffsetunits
parameters.label: Text Offset Units
parameters.description: Determine the units used by the Text Offset parameter.

parameters.name: textpaddingunits
parameters.label: Text Padding Units
parameters.description: The units used by the text padding.

parameters.name: alignx
parameters.label: Horizontal Align
parameters.description: Determines how the text is positioned horizontally in the panel. For example, with left alignment, the text will be positioned against the left side of the panel plus any defined padding.

parameters.name: alignxmode
parameters.label: Horizontal Align Mode
parameters.description: Determines how the text is measured for calculating the alignment. Only font metrics are available when using Multiline or Specification mode.

parameters.name: aligny
parameters.label: Vertical Align
parameters.description: Determines how the text is positioned vertically inside the panel.

parameters.name: alignymode
parameters.label: Vertical Align Mode
parameters.description: Determines how the text is measured when doing the vertical alignment.

parameters.name: x
parameters.label: X
parameters.description: Specify the horizontal position in pixels relative to its parent.

parameters.name: y
parameters.label: Y
parameters.description: Specify the vertical position in pixels relative to its parent.

parameters.name: w
parameters.label: Width
parameters.description: Specify the panel's width in pixels.

parameters.name: h
parameters.label: Height
parameters.description: Specify the 's height in pixels.

parameters.name: fixedaspect
parameters.label: Fixed Aspect
parameters.description: Allows easy creation of panels with a specific aspect set in the Aspect Ratio parameter below. Only requires setting the width or height of the panel and the other dimension is calculated based on the

parameters.name: aspect
parameters.label: Aspect Ratio
parameters.description: Specify the ratio when using Fixed Aspect parameter above, the ratio is width/height.

parameters.name: layer
parameters.label: Depth Layer
parameters.description: Specifies the order the panel components are drawn in, similar to layers in Photoshop. Higher values will be drawn over any other panel with a lower value (that is at the same level of hierarchy). If

parameters.name: hmode
parameters.label: Horizontal Mode
parameters.description: Select one of 3 modes to determine the horizontal width of the panel.

parameters.name: leftanchor
parameters.label: Left Anchor
parameters.description: Position of the left anchor of the panel with respect to the parent. This value is normalized 0-1, 0 is the left edge of the parent and 1 is the right edge of the parent.

parameters.name: leftoffset
parameters.label: Left Offset
parameters.description: An offset for the left anchor in pixels.

parameters.name: rightanchor
parameters.label: Right Anchor
parameters.description: Position of the right anchor of the panel with respect to the parent. This value is normalized 0-1, 0 is the left edge of the parent and 1 is the right edge of the parent.

parameters.name: rightoffset
parameters.label: Right Offset
parameters.description: An offset for the right anchor in pixels.

parameters.name: horigin
parameters.label: Horizontal Origin
parameters.description: Sets the position of the panel's origin horizontally. The default origin (0,0) is the bottom-left corner of the panel.

parameters.name: hfillweight
parameters.label: Horizontal Fill Weight
parameters.description: When multiple panels are using Horizontal Mode = Fill and being aligned by the parent either Left to Right or Right to Left, this fill weight parameter can be used to bias the fill width of the panels

parameters.name: vmode
parameters.label: Vertical Mode
parameters.description: Select one of 3 modes to determine the vertical height of the panel.

parameters.name: bottomanchor
parameters.label: Bottom Anchor
parameters.description: Position of the bottom anchor of the panel with respect to the parent. This value is normalized 0-1, 0 is the bottom edge of the parent and 1 is the top edge of the parent.

parameters.name: bottomoffset
parameters.label: Bottom Offset
parameters.description: An offset for the bottom anchor in pixels.

parameters.name: topanchor
parameters.label: Top Anchor
parameters.description: Position of the top anchor of the panel with respect to the parent. This value is normalized 0-1, 0 is the bottom edge of the parent and 1 is the top edge of the parent.

parameters.name: topoffset
parameters.label: Top Offset
parameters.description: An offset for the top anchor in pixels.

parameters.name: vorigin
parameters.label: Vertical Origin
parameters.description: Sets the position of the panel's origin vertically. The default origin (0,0) is the bottom-left corner of the panel.

parameters.name: vfillweight
parameters.label: Vertical Fill Weight
parameters.description: When multiple panels are using Vertical Mode = Fill and being aligned by the parent either Top to Bottom or Bottom to Top, this fill weight parameter can be used to bias the fill height of the panels.

parameters.name: alignallow
parameters.label: Parent Alignment
parameters.description: When set to Ignore, the Panel will ignore any Align parameter settings from its parent.

parameters.name: alignorder
parameters.label: Align Order
parameters.description: This parameter allows you to specify the align position when its parent's Align parameter is set to something other then or . Lower numbers are first.

parameters.name: sizefromwindow
parameters.label: Size from Window
parameters.description: When enabled the panel component's width and height are set by resizing its floating viewer window.

parameters.name: display
parameters.label: Display
parameters.description: Specifies if the panel is displayed or hidden. Changing this parameter may incur some layout processing costs.  For simple cases, such as overlays it is more performant to adjust the opacity parameter

parameters.name: enable
parameters.label: Enable
parameters.description: Allows you to prevent all interaction with this panel.

parameters.name: helpdat
parameters.label: Help DAT
parameters.description: Lets you specify the path to a whose content will be displayed as a rollover pop-up help for the control panel.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.label: Cursor
parameters.description: Changes the cursor displayed when cursor is over the panel.

parameters.name: multitouch
parameters.label: Multi-Touch
parameters.description: When enabled, this panel will process the first touch it gets in a similar manner to how it processes a mouse click, with updates to u, v, state etc.  The touch event must be initiated from the panel.

parameters.name: constraincursor
parameters.label: Constrain Cursor
parameters.description: Constrains the cursor to this panel, keeping it inside once it enters.

parameters.name: clickthrough
parameters.label: Click Through
parameters.description: When enabled all mouse clicks are ignored by this .

parameters.name: mousewheel
parameters.label: Use Mouse Wheel
parameters.description: Turn on to capture events when the mouse wheel is used over the panel.

parameters.name: mouserel
parameters.label: Relative UV
parameters.description: When enabled the uv will reflect relative mouse movement.

parameters.name: reposition
parameters.label: Drag to Reposition
parameters.description: Enables repositioning of the panel or window by dragging with the mouse.

parameters.name: repocomp
parameters.label: Component
parameters.description: Enabled by choosing the option from the Reposition parameter. Specify the path to the panel component you would like to reposition by mouse.

parameters.name: anchordrag
parameters.label: Anchor Drag
parameters.description: When Drag To Reposition parameter is set to , and the panel's Horizontal Mode and/or Vertical Mode is set to Anchors, this menu determines whether drag-to-reposition actions change Anchor values or Of

parameters.name: scrolloverlay
parameters.label: Scroll Overlay
parameters.description: Controls whether the panel is affected by scrollbar position. This allows the creation of panel overlays that aren't affected by the panel's scrollbars.

parameters.name: bgalpha
parameters.label: Background Alpha
parameters.description: Set the alpha value for the background.

parameters.name: top
parameters.label: Background TOP
parameters.description: Allows you to specify a as the background for the panel.

parameters.name: topfill
parameters.label: TOP Fill
parameters.description: This menu specifies the way the Background will fill the panel's background.

parameters.name: topsmoothness
parameters.label: TOP Smoothness
parameters.description: This menu controls background 's viewer smoothness settings. In previous builds of TouchDesigner, this was always 'Mipmap Pixels', so old files will load with this setting whereas the default for new

parameters.name: borderaalpha
parameters.label: Border A Alpha
parameters.description: Alpha value for border A color.

parameters.name: borderbalpha
parameters.label: Border B Alpha
parameters.description: Alpha value for border B color.

parameters.name: leftborder
parameters.label: Left Border
parameters.description: What color the 2 left-most pixels are. Options are 0 (no change), Border A (uses color defined in Border A), or Border B (uses color defined in Border B).

parameters.name: leftborderi
parameters.label: Left Border Inside
parameters.description: Same as above parameter but used for an inside border.

parameters.name: rightborder
parameters.label: Right Border
parameters.description: What color the 2 right-most pixels are. Options are 0 (no change), Border A (uses color defined in Border A), or Border B (uses color defined in Border B).

parameters.name: rightborderi
parameters.label: Right Border Inside
parameters.description: Same as above parameter but used for an inside border.

parameters.name: bottomborder
parameters.label: Bottom Border
parameters.description: What color the 2 bottom-most pixels are. Options are 0 (no change), Border A (uses color defined in Border A), or Border B (uses color defined in Border B).

parameters.name: bottomborderi
parameters.label: Bottom Border Inside
parameters.description: Same as above parameter but used for an inside border.

parameters.name: topborder
parameters.label: Top Border
parameters.description: What color the 2 top-most pixels are. Options are 0 (no change), Border A (uses color defined in Border A), or Border B (uses color defined in Border B).

parameters.name: topborderi
parameters.label: Top Border Inside
parameters.description: Same as above parameter but used for an inside border.

parameters.name: borderover
parameters.label: Border Over Children
parameters.description: Draws the panel's borders on top of all children panels.

parameters.name: dodisablecolor
parameters.label: Disable Color
parameters.description: Enable the use of a unique disable color below when the panel's Enable = Off.

parameters.name: disablealpha
parameters.label: Disable Alpha
parameters.description: Set the alpha value for the disable color.

parameters.name: multrgb
parameters.label: Multiply RGB by Alpha
parameters.description: Multiplies the RGB channels by the alpha channel.

parameters.name: composite
parameters.label: Composite
parameters.description: Selects how the panel is composited with its siblings panels. See the for a description of the various composite methods.

parameters.name: opacity
parameters.label: Opacity
parameters.description: Allows you to control the transparency of the panel.

parameters.name: align
parameters.label: Align
parameters.description: This menu allows you to specify how the children inside the will be laid out. The options , and will scale the 's children to fit the . They use the Align Order of each of the children to determine th

parameters.name: spacing
parameters.label: Spacing
parameters.description: This is enabled by choosing any Align option other than or . It defines the space between the children when they are being aligned.

parameters.name: alignmax
parameters.label: Max per Line
parameters.description: This is enabled by choosing any Align option other than , , , or , and defines the maximum number of children placed in one row or column.

parameters.name: justifymethod
parameters.label: Justify Mathod
parameters.description: This menu specifies if the panel's children are being justified as a group or individually using the Jusitfy Horizontal / Vertical parameters below.

parameters.name: justifyh
parameters.label: Justify Horizontal
parameters.description: This menu specifies if the panel's children are being justified horizontally.

parameters.name: justifyv
parameters.label: Justify Vertical
parameters.description: This menu specifies if the panel's children are being justified vertically.

parameters.name: fit
parameters.label: Fit
parameters.description: This menu allows you to scale the panel's children. It overrides the Justify Horizontal and Justify Vertical parameters.

parameters.name: crop
parameters.label: Crop
parameters.description: This menu determines if any children panels which are positioned partially or completely outside the panel component's dimensions get cropped.

parameters.name: phscrollbar
parameters.label: Horizontal Scrollbar
parameters.description: Setting for horizontal scrollbar on this panel.

parameters.name: pvscrollbar
parameters.label: Vertical Scrollbar
parameters.description: Setting for vertical scrollbar on this panel.

parameters.name: scrollbarthickness
parameters.label: Thickness
parameters.description: Set the thickness of the scrollbars in pixels.

parameters.name: drag
parameters.label: When Dragging This
parameters.description: Specify if this can be dragged.

parameters.name: dragscript
parameters.label: Drag Script
parameters.description: Specify a script that will be executed when starting to drag a . Please refer to the Drag section of the page.

parameters.name: droptypescript
parameters.label: Drop Types
parameters.description: If a drop destination script is specified, you can also add a table with a list of return types that the drop destination script will provide. Return types can be one of the op types ( , , , , , ), ch

parameters.name: dropdestscript
parameters.label: Drop Destination Script
parameters.description: Specify a script that will be executed when the dragged is dropped. A temporary network is created and the component (or the alternative operator specified in Dropped ) is copied to this network. You

parameters.name: paneldragop
parameters.label: Dropped Operator
parameters.description: The Dropped parameter is the easiest way to specify an alternative operator to drop. Note that this alternative operator must exist, otherwise the component itself will be dropped. The alternative is

parameters.name: drop
parameters.label: On Dropping Into
parameters.description: Specify if this accepts items that are dropped onto it.

parameters.name: builtindrop
parameters.label: Built-In Drop Options
parameters.description: Normally, when dropping over a node, if the viewer is not active, built-in drop options are provided, otherwise custom defined drop options are provided.  When this option is off, custom defined drop

parameters.name: dropscript
parameters.label: Drop Script
parameters.description: A component's Drop is run when you drop another component or an external file into that component. Please refer to the Drop Scripts - Text section of the page.

parameters.name: dropparm
parameters.label: Drop Parameter
parameters.description: When 'On Dropping Into' is set to

parameters.name: dragdropcallbacks
parameters.label: Drag/Drop Callbacks
parameters.description: Specify which holds the custom drag/drop scripts.  If blank, press 'Add' to create a with default scripts.

parameters.name: reinitextensions
parameters.label: Re-Init Extensions
parameters.description: Recompile all extension objects. Normally extension objects are compiled only when they are referenced and their definitions have changed.

parameters.name: initextonstart
parameters.label: Init Extensions On Start
parameters.description: Perform a Re-Init automatically when TouchDEsigner Starts

parameters.name: ext
parameters.label: Extension
parameters.description: Sequence of info for creating extensions on this component

parameters.name: ext0object
parameters.label: Object
parameters.description: A number of class instances that can be attached to the component.

parameters.name: ext0name
parameters.label: Name
parameters.description: Optional name to search by, instead of the instance class name.

parameters.name: ext0promote
parameters.label: Promote
parameters.description: Controls whether or not the extensions are visible directly at the component level, or must be accessed through the member.  Example: vs

parameters.name: parentshortcut
parameters.label: Parent Shortcut
parameters.description: Specifies a name you can use anywhere inside the component as the path to that component. See .

parameters.name: opshortcut
parameters.label: Global OP Shortcut
parameters.description: Specifies a name you can use anywhere at all as the path to that component. See .

parameters.name: iop
parameters.label: Internal OP
parameters.description: Sequence header for internal operators.

parameters.name: iop0shortcut
parameters.label: Shortcut
parameters.description: Specifies a name you can use anywhere inside the component as a path to "Internal " below. See .

parameters.name: iop0op
parameters.label: OP
parameters.description: The path to the Internal OP inside this component. See .

parameters.name: nodeview
parameters.label: Node View
parameters.description: Determines what is displayed in the node viewer, also known as the . Some options will not be available depending on the type ( , , Misc.)

parameters.name: opviewer
parameters.label: Operator Viewer
parameters.description: Select which operator's node viewer to use when the Node View parameter above is set to Operator .

parameters.name: enablecloning
parameters.label: Enable Cloning
parameters.description: Control if the OP should be actively cloneing. Turning this off causes this node to stop cloning it's 'Clone Master'.

parameters.name: enablecloningpulse
parameters.label: Enable Cloning Pulse
parameters.description: Instantaneously clone the contents.

parameters.name: clone
parameters.label: Clone Master
parameters.description: to a component used as the Master .

parameters.name: loadondemand
parameters.label: Load on Demand
parameters.description: Loads the component into memory only when required. Good to use for components that are not always used in the project.

parameters.name: enableexternaltox
parameters.label: Enable External .tox
parameters.description: When on (default), the external file will be loaded when the starts and the contents of the will match that of the external . This can be turned off to avoid loading from the referenced external on st

parameters.name: enableexternaltoxpulse
parameters.label: Enable External .tox Pulse
parameters.description: This button will re-load from the external file (if present).

parameters.name: externaltox
parameters.label: External .tox Path
parameters.description: to a file on disk which will source the component's contents upon start of a . This allows for components to contain networks that can be updated independently. If the file can not be found, whatever

parameters.name: reloadcustom
parameters.label: Reload Custom Parameters
parameters.description: When this checkbox is enabled, the values of the component's are reloaded when the is reloaded. This only affects top-level parameters on the component, all parameters on nodes inside the component ar

parameters.name: reloadbuiltin
parameters.label: Reload Built-In Parameters
parameters.description: When this checkbox is enabled, the values of the component's built-in parameters are reloaded when the is reloaded. This only affects top-level parameters on the component, all parameters on nodes ins

parameters.name: savebackup
parameters.label: Save Backup of External
parameters.description: When this checkbox is enabled, a backup copy of the component specified by the External parameter is saved in the file.  This backup copy will be used if the External can not be found. This may happen

parameters.name: subcompname
parameters.label: Sub-Component to Load
parameters.description: When loading from an External file, this option allows you to reach into the and pull out a and make that the top-level , ignoring everything else in the file (except for the contents of that ). For e

parameters.name: relpath
parameters.label: Relative File Path Behavior
parameters.description: Set whether the child file paths within this are relative to the itself or the , or inherit from parent.

parameters.name: uvbuttonsright
parameters.label: Uvbuttonsright
parameters.description: Allows you to specify which mouse buttons update the uv Panel Values.

parameters.name: resizecomp
parameters.label: Resizecomp
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: scaley
parameters.label: Scaley
parameters.description: Allows you to uniformly scale the Panel's children.

parameters.name: fontcolorb
parameters.label: Fontcolorb
parameters.description: The default color of the text. Additional colors can be applied to separate parts of the text using formatting codes.

parameters.name: dropshadowoffsety
parameters.label: Dropshadowoffsety
parameters.description: An offset applied to the shadow position after any other alignment operations. Positive values move the text up and to the right.

parameters.name: repositionymax
parameters.label: Repositionymax
parameters.description: Enabled by choosing the Component option from the Reposition parameter.  Sets the maximum range for repositioning the panel component vertically.

parameters.name: dropshadowcolorb
parameters.label: Dropshadowcolorb
parameters.description: The color of the drop shadow. Additional colors can be applied to separate parts of the text using formatting codes.

parameters.name: offsety
parameters.label: Offsety
parameters.description: Allows you to offset the Panel's children. This parameter is overwritten by the Align, Justify Horizontal, and Justify Vertical parameters above.

parameters.name: bgcolorb
parameters.label: Bgcolorb
parameters.description: RGB values for the background. (default: black (0,0,0))

parameters.name: textoffsety
parameters.label: Textoffsety
parameters.description: An offset applied to the text position after any other alignment operations. Positive values move the text up and to the right.

parameters.name: selectcolorb
parameters.label: Selectcolorb
parameters.description: The color of the selection bar when Custom Select Color is enabled.

parameters.name: repositionxmax
parameters.label: Repositionxmax
parameters.description: Enabled by choosing the Component option from the Reposition parameter.  Sets the maximum range for  repositioning the panel component horizontally.

parameters.name: placeholdercolorb
parameters.label: Placeholdercolorb
parameters.description: The default color of the placeholder text. Additional colors can be applied to separate parts of the text using formatting codes.

parameters.name: resizehmax
parameters.label: Resizehmax
parameters.description: Limits the bottom-top (height) resizing range.

parameters.name: resizewmax
parameters.label: Resizewmax
parameters.description: Limits the left-right (width) resizing range.

parameters.name: postoffsety
parameters.label: Postoffsety
parameters.description: Adds an offset after all other postions and alignment options have been applied to the panel.

parameters.name: textpaddingt
parameters.label: Textpaddingt
parameters.description: Set the amount of space between the edges of the panel and the text. Whether the padding has an effect on the position of the text depends on the current alignment mode and whether word wrapping is us

parameters.name: borderab
parameters.label: Borderab
parameters.description: RGB values for border A color.

parameters.name: allowuishortcuts
parameters.label: Allowuishortcuts
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: resizet
parameters.label: Resizet
parameters.description: Four checkboxes allow you to enable resizing a panel by grabbing the corresponding edge or corner: Resize Left, Right, Bottom, Top.

parameters.name: borderbb
parameters.label: Borderbb
parameters.description: RGBA values for border B color.

parameters.name: disablecolorb
parameters.label: Disablecolorb
parameters.description: RGB values for the disable color. (default: black (0,0,0))

parameters.name: margint
parameters.label: Margint
parameters.description: The four fields allow you to specify the space that surrounds the Panel Component. The margin is the space between the Panel Component's border and the outer edge. The Margin is defined in absolute pi

examples.label: Configure Border Over Children and Legacy Font Selection
examples.code:
n = op('text1')
n.par.borderover = False
n.par.legacyfontselection = True
n.par.text = 'The quick brown fox jumped over the bush.'
examples.description: Common settings from 17 real Derivative examples

examples.label: Alternative configuration
examples.code:
n = op('text1')
n.par.borderover = False
n.par.legacyfontselection = True
